text: hell’s going on, Ronin ?” “We need to leave,” he replied . “You said that already .” “He knows you’re here, Lara.” He turned to face her. The color had drained from her face. “He doesn’t know it’s you , I don’t think, but that doesn’t matter. We have to gather everything that’s essential, and get out of here .” He held up the smaller of the two coats to compare it to her body. It was still much too large, but it would be her only real protection from the unforgiving weather . “Shouldn’t we at least wait until dark?” She took the coat from him and hugged it to her chest . “No.” Swinging the other coat on, he returned to the bed and picked up the pistol, offering it to Lara grip-first. “It needs to be now . We’ll cut through the backyards until we have to cross the street, then swing south, through the slums until we’re out of sight of the wall. Then we’ll cut west .” “What about the guards ?” “They’re only posted at the gates, most of the time .” “And we’re going over the wall .” He nodded and raised his hand slightly. “ Take it .” She closed her fingers around the pistol’s grip and stared at it. “I’ll…go get ready .” As she walked away, Ronin put his hand on her upper arm, turning her toward him. Stepping close, he cradled her face in his hands and pressed his forehead against hers. His immense relief at seeing her unharmed wouldn’t mean anything until Cheyenne was far behind him, but he could relish a single moment of togetherness, of her beauty . “I’ll keep you safe, Lara Brooks .” She smiled, her breath tickling his skin, and placed a hand on his forearm. “ I know .” “Pack as much food as you can. Focus on anything that’ll last more than a day or two. And fill as many containers as you can find with water .” “Okay.” Lara withdrew, coat still draped over one arm, and left the room . He didn’t turn to his gear until she reached the staircase and exited his field of vision . * * * “R eady?” Ronin asked . Lara tilted her head back to stare up at the wall. She’d only seen it from the outside, where the mismatched pieces somehow came together to form an almost-sheer face. It couldn’t be called pretty by any stretch, but this side was far worse. There’d been no attempt at uniformity; pieces of metal sheeting and piping jutted out of it in some places, wooden slats and chunks of concrete in others . Seeing it so close, with light still in the sky, made the rest of the Bot District feel uglier. Like all its cleanliness was just a false front . After cutting through alleys and backyards to avoid gearheads, this was supposed to be the easy part. But from here, the wall seemed likely to collapse and crush her to death . “ Ready .” Ronin dropped to a knee and laced his fingers together. “I’ll pass up your bag, once you’re on top .” She nodded, setting her pack down and placing her foot on his hands. As he stood, she steadied herself by holding his shoulders. When he shifted a hand beneath each of her feet, she used the wall for stability, trusting her own balance less than she trusted him . The top of the wall was at her waist. She found handholds and lifted a leg over it, grateful for the padding of the coat as a metal edge pressed against her stomach. Within a few seconds, she was straddling the wall . Ronin handed the bag up. She took it and settled it between her thighs. After checking the area behind him, he passed her the rifle, which she laid over the bag and held securely in place. Then, despite carrying two packs full of scrap, his belt of tools, their ammunition, and another bag full of water containers, he climbed with little apparent difficulty . The wall groaned as he neared the top. Lara sucked in a breath, hands tightening on the rifle . He pulled himself up, a piece of concrete breaking off his last foothold. It landed in the dirt below with a dull thump . His eyes met hers for a moment, his lips lifting in a little smile, and then he searched the area again. Lara did the same; she’d never seen the human shacks from this angle. The contrast between the opposite sides of the wall was stark . Within the barrier, everything was clean and deceptively alive. Orderly and precise. Green trees along the streets, electric lamps only now flickering on, well-maintained rooftops. Beyond the wall, the landscape was bleak, a smattering of browns and yellows. The only plant life was the scrub grass poking up through the dust. Clumped together with no pattern, the human shacks were small, rickety, filthy things . The sun was slipping away on the western horizon. It would be dark soon, and they’d be out there, in the shadows … “Looks clear.” Ronin leapt down, dust swirling up around his boots as he landed. He held his arms up toward Lara . She lowered the rifle, and once he had it strapped over his shoulder, she passed down her bag. Ronin set it at his feet and raised his arms once more. Without hesitation, Lara swung her other leg over the top of the wall and pushed off . He caught her easily, holding her against him . “Hard part’s done,” he said, finally . “Seemed simple enough,” she replied, smirking . “Chance worked in our favor. We just need to pass through there,” Ronin tipped his head toward the shacks she used to call home, “and we’ll be in Cheyenne no more.” He set her gently on her feet and bent to retrieve
